description_long: This object is an egg-shaped item with a textured, slightly rough surface that appears to be made of a hard shell. Its exterior is predominantly a deep reddish-brown color, mottled with irregular patches of lighter, yellowish-brown markings that give it a natural, speckled appearance. The surface has a matte to slightly grippy finish, with visible micro-texture that suggests a tactile, non-glossy feel. The egg is uniformly oval, with no visible cracks, openings, or additional features, and the coloration is consistent across all sides. The size and proportions are typical of a large bird egg, such as those from a chicken or similar species. The overall impression is of a robust, natural egg that could be found in an outdoor or urban environment.
description: A large, oval egg with a hard, reddish-brown shell covered in irregular yellowish-brown speckles and a textured, matte finish.
description_view_0: The egg's surface is dominated by reddish-brown with prominent yellowish-brown mottling, showing a slightly more concentrated patch pattern on this side.
description_view_1: This view reveals a more even distribution of the yellowish-brown speckles, with the texture and coloration appearing consistent and natural.
description_view_2: The speckled pattern is slightly lighter and more diffuse, with the reddish-brown base color still prominent.
description_view_3: This side displays the most extensive yellowish-brown coverage, with the mottling appearing denser and more blended into the base color.
attribute_orientation: upright with the broader end at the bottom and the narrower end at the top
attribute_contextual_placement: Most likely encountered as a natural egg in a nest or as a found object in an urban park or garden setting.
attribute_decorative_patterns: irregular yellowish-brown mottling on a reddish-brown base
